title: Sour Egyptian Fish Soup
description: A light, tangy fish soup infused with white wine, lemon, and herbs—perfect for a quick, low‑calorie meal on a sick day.

ingredients.name: firm white fish fillets (cod or tilapia)

instructions: Heat olive oil in a large pot over medium heat.
Add chopped onion and sauté until translucent, about 3 minutes.
Add minced garlic and cook for another 30 seconds until fragrant.
Stir in diced tomatoes, cumin, and paprika; cook for 5 minutes until tomatoes soften.
Pour in the reduced white wine and allow it to simmer for 2 minutes, reducing slightly.
Add fish stock, water, lemon juice, and white wine vinegar; bring the mixture to a gentle boil.
Reduce heat, then add the fish fillets, breaking them into bite‑sized pieces; simmer until fish flakes easily, about 6-8 minutes.
Season with salt, pepper, dill, and parsley; adjust seasoning if needed.
Serve hot, spooning out the broth with a spoonful of flaky fish and herbs.
